title: Ensure Essential Contacts is Configured for Organization

input_row.title: Ensure Essential Contacts is Configured for Organization
input_row.description: It is recommended that Essential Contacts is configured to designate email addresses for Google Cloud services to notify of important technical or security information.
input_row.rationale: Many Google Cloud services, such as Cloud Billing, send out notifications to share important information with Google Cloud users. By default, these notifications are sent to members with certain Identity and Access Management (IAM) roles. With Essential Contacts, you can customize who receives notifications by providing your own list of contacts.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Essential Contacts by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/essential-contacts 2. Make sure the organization appears in the resource selector at the top of the page. The resource selector tells you what project, folder, or organization you are currently managing contacts for. 3. Ensure that appropriate email addresses are configured for each of the following notification categories: • Legal • Security • Suspension • Technical Alternatively, appropriate email addresses can be configured for the All notification category to receive all possible important notifications. From Google Cloud CLI 1. To list all configured organization Essential Contacts run a command: gcloud essential-contacts list --organization= 2. Ensure at least one appropriate email address is configured for each of the following notification categories: • LEGAL • SECURITY • SUSPENSION • TECHNICAL Alternatively, appropriate email addresses can be configured for the ALL notification category to receive all possible important notifications.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Essential Contacts by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/essential-contacts 2. Make sure the organization appears in the resource selector at the top of the page. The resource selector tells you what project, folder, or organization you are currently managing contacts for. 3. Click +Add contact 4. In the Email and Confirm Email fields, enter the email address of the contact. 5. From the Notification categories drop-down menu, select the notification categories that you want the contact to receive communications for. 6. Click Save From Google Cloud CLI 1. To add an organization Essential Contacts run a command: gcloud essential-contacts create --email="" \ --notification-categories="" \ --organization=
input_row.impact: There is no charge for Essential Contacts except for the 'Technical Incidents' category that is only available to premium support customers.
input_row.default_value: By default, there are no Essential Contacts configured. In the absence of an Essential Contact, the following IAM roles are used to identify users to notify for the following categories: • Legal: roles/billing.admin • Security: roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin • Suspension: roles/owner • Technical: roles/owner • Technical Incidents: roles/owner

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field provides specific GCP CLI commands (gcloud essential-contacts list) that can be used to check the configuration of Essential Contacts within an organization, supporting the decision that this control can be automated.
- Essential Contacts is a standard GCP service feature that is API-checkable and relevant to notifications concerning IAM roles and organization security.
- GCP CLI commands are provided directly in the audit, confirming that the verification can be automated.
- There is no requirement for manual checks or human judgment, which further supports the automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_organization_essential_contacts_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the GCP CLI command `gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<organization_id>` to list all configured essential contacts for the organization.
- Enumerate all available notification categories (Legal, Security, Suspension, Technical, All) to determine if appropriate email addresses are configured for each category.
- Validate that at least one email address is set for each specific category or that the "All" category is used.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: At least one appropriate email configured for each of the specified categories (Legal, Security, Suspension, Technical).
  - Fail: No email is configured for any of the specified categories or there are no essential contacts set up at all.
- Check against the default value which indicates that there are no Essential Contacts configured by default, ensuring compliance if configurations are present.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of this control as Automated is correct. The configuration of Essential Contacts can be verified using the GCP CLI, specifically with the `gcloud essential-contacts list` command. This command allows for the retrieval of configured contacts, making it suitable for automation without the need for manual intervention.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The command `gcloud essential-contacts list --organization=<organization_id>` is valid and can be used to list Essential Contacts for an organization.
- The criteria for passing or failing the control are clear and align with the control's requirements.
- The program name `gcp_organization_essential_contacts_configured` is somewhat descriptive, but it could be more specific about the compliance aspect being checked.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved for clarity. A suggestion would be `gcp_essential_contacts_notification_categories_configured`, which specifies that the check is for notification categories.
- The assessment could benefit from mentioning the potential use of the GCP API directly for more advanced automation scenarios, such as integrating with other compliance tools or dashboards.
- Consider verifying the permissions required to execute the `gcloud essential-contacts list` command, ensuring that the audit process accounts for necessary IAM roles or permissions.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded, and the use of the GCP CLI for this control is appropriate. The details provided are technically sound, and the automation approach is justified.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `gcp_essential_contacts_notification_categories_configured` for better clarity and specificity.
- The rest of the assessment can remain as-is, as it accurately reflects the capabilities of the GCP CLI and the requirements of the control.
